solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int minimizedStringLength(string s) {
        return set<char>(s.begin(), s.end()).size();
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The problem reduces to counting unique characters since each duplicate can be eliminated via the allowed operations. Using a set to track unique characters is optimal. Time complexity is O(n log n) due to set insertion overhead, and space is O(1) as there are at most 26 unique lowercase letters.
